title: Distinguish data drift from pipeline bugs
description: When a distribution shifts, determine whether the world changed or the pipeline broke before alerting or retraining.
body: Use when a monitored distribution or metric moves unexpectedly. Do not assume a shift means genuine drift (warranting retraining) — a join change, unit change, or partial load produces the same symptom and a different fix. Actions: compare the suspect feature against its baseline, check upstream for schema/logic changes and load completeness, and confirm row counts before concluding drift. Validation signals include a drift metric tied to a baseline plus a clean pipeline-integrity check. Risk boundary: retraining on a pipeline bug bakes the bug into the model; rule out a data defect before attributing a shift to real drift.
